visa_types.name: Long-term stay
visa_types.documents_required:
Applications can be made online or handed in at VFS offices. It is encouraged by both DIS and SIRI that applications be made online, since the digital application form adapts to the answers and automatically goes to the relevant institution when you submit it.
- For online applications, the documents in following checklist should be submitted at VFS: (
Online Applications Checklist
)
- For hand-in applications, the documents in following checklist should be submitted at VFS: (
hand-in application checklist
).
- For Re-Entry Permit Applications, the documents in following checklist should be submitted at VFS:
 
Re entry permit application form / Re entry permit application checklist
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
Please remember that in hand-in applications, the applicant is responsible for submitting the required documents mentioned on the relevant application form.
Applications can be made in the following categories.
Family Reunification
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link:https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family)
Accompanying Family Members (to a person working or studying in Denmark)
For details on how to apply and required documents please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Accompanying-family-members (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Accompanying-family-members)
Work
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work)
Researchers
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Researcher (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Researcher)
Trainee
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Traineeer (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Trainee)
Study
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Study (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Study)
Internship
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Interns (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Interns)
Au Pair
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Au-pair (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Au-pair)
Religious Worker
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Religous-worker (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Religous-worker)
Faroe Islands and Greenland
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/The-Faroe-Islands-and-Greenland (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/The-Faroe-Islands-and-Greenland)
Note that this type of application is not available online. It must be handed in at VFS and the hand-in application checklist above is applicable.
APPLICATIONS MADE IN DENMARK
For applications made in Denmark, applicants receive letters requesting biometrics (not to be confused with online application receipts) or decisions. In such situations, the following checklist is applicable when going to VFS: Biometrics Checklist and Decisions Checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
FEE LIST

[Table:  Long-term stay]
Note: From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/) (except for re-entry permit applications and decision applications. Please check relevant checklists for payment for these).
The receipt for the payment must be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics.
The Embassy fee and the fee to the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) must be paid in addition to the fee to The Danish Immigration Service (DIS)/ Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI).
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive 16% VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
- The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in Kazakhstani Tenge.
- Please note Applicants applying in Almaty need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 12 (6755 KZT) it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ankara, Turkey back to Almaty, Kazakhstan
- Please Note:
- ‘If a person submits an application, appeal or request to reopen a case without paying the fee on the belief that he/she is exempt from paying the fee and the relevant immigration authorities later determine that the individual was, in fact, not exempt, the application appeal/request will be rejected. In such instances, the person will need to go through the entire process again: create case order ID, pay the fee, and submit a new application/appeal/request. If a person has paid the fee, but the relevant immigration authorities find that he/she was, in fact, exempt, the fee will be refunded. Note that the Embassy cannot decide on whether you are exempt or not.’
visa_types.processing_time: DIS and SIRI have set maximum expected processing times for different application types. This period is specified under each respective application type and you can check it from the links given under ‘documents required’.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to stay in Denmark longer than 90 days for the purposes mentioned in the list below, you must apply for a residence and work permit. Applicants for Residence and Work Permit prepare and submit their application as per their own discretion using all available information in the respective links below.
- Work (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work)
- Accompanying family members (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Accompanying-family-members)
- Researchers (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Researcher)
- Trainees (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Trainee)
- Religious workers (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Religous-worker)
- Studies (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Study)
- Au pairs (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Au-pair)
- Interns (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Interns)
- Family Reunification (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification)
- Greenland and Faroe Islands (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/The-Faroe-Islands-and-Greenland)
- Re-Entry Permit (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Reentry-permit)
- Applications for residence permits are processed and decided by either the Danish Immigration Service (DIS) or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) depending on the type of application.
- DENMARK INTRODUCES BIOMETRIC RESIDENCE CARDS 20 MAY 2012
- From 20 May 2012 residence cards must contain digital photos, fingerprints and signatures. In this way the security against forgery and misuse of residence cards is strengthened.
- When you come to the visa application centre to deliver a residence/ work permit application, you will need to have your biometric data recorded. You should remove any henna on your fingertips, or allow it to fade, as this may prevent us from getting a clear scan.
- Every applicant who is submitting a residence permit application must attend the visa application centre in person. Children of less than 6 years need to attend, but are not require to have fingerprints or signature recorded, only a photograph.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to stay in Denmark longer than 90 days for the purposes mentioned in the list below, you must apply for a residence and work permit. Applicants for Residence and Work Permit prepare and submit their application as per their own discretion using all available information in the respective links below.
- Work (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work)
- Accompanying family members (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Accompanying-family-members)
- Researchers (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Researcher)
- Trainees (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Trainee)
- Religious workers (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Religous-worker)
- Studies (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Study)
- Au pairs (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Au-pair)
- Interns (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Interns)
- Family Reunification (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification)
- Greenland and Faroe Islands (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/The-Faroe-Islands-and-Greenland)
- Re-Entry Permit (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Reentry-permit)
- Applications for residence permits are processed and decided by either the Danish Immigration Service (DIS) or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) depending on the type of application.
- DENMARK INTRODUCES BIOMETRIC RESIDENCE CARDS 20 MAY 2012
- From 20 May 2012 residence cards must contain digital photos, fingerprints and signatures. In this way the security against forgery and misuse of residence cards is strengthened.
- When you come to the visa application centre to deliver a residence/ work permit application, you will need to have your biometric data recorded. You should remove any henna on your fingertips, or allow it to fade, as this may prevent us from getting a clear scan.
- Every applicant who is submitting a residence permit application must attend the visa application centre in person. Children of less than 6 years need to attend, but are not require to have fingerprints or signature recorded, only a photograph.

## Visa Fees
FEE LIST

[Table:  Long-term stay]
Note: From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/) (except for re-entry permit applications and decision applications. Please check relevant checklists for payment for these).
The receipt for the payment must be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics.
The Embassy fee and the fee to the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) must be paid in addition to the fee to The Danish Immigration Service (DIS)/ Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI).
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive 16% VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
- The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in Kazakhstani Tenge.
- Please note Applicants applying in Almaty need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 12 (6755 KZT) it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ankara, Turkey back to Almaty, Kazakhstan
- Please Note:
- ‘If a person submits an application, appeal or request to reopen a case without paying the fee on the belief that he/she is exempt from paying the fee and the relevant immigration authorities later determine that the individual was, in fact, not exempt, the application appeal/request will be rejected. In such instances, the person will need to go through the entire process again: create case order ID, pay the fee, and submit a new application/appeal/request. If a person has paid the fee, but the relevant immigration authorities find that he/she was, in fact, exempt, the fee will be refunded. Note that the Embassy cannot decide on whether you are exempt or not.’

## Documents Required
Applications can be made online or handed in at VFS offices. It is encouraged by both DIS and SIRI that applications be made online, since the digital application form adapts to the answers and automatically goes to the relevant institution when you submit it.
- For online applications, the documents in following checklist should be submitted at VFS: (
Online Applications Checklist
)
- For hand-in applications, the documents in following checklist should be submitted at VFS: (
hand-in application checklist
).
- For Re-Entry Permit Applications, the documents in following checklist should be submitted at VFS:
 
Re entry permit application form / Re entry permit application checklist
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
Please remember that in hand-in applications, the applicant is responsible for submitting the required documents mentioned on the relevant application form.
Applications can be made in the following categories.
Family Reunification
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link:https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family)
Accompanying Family Members (to a person working or studying in Denmark)
For details on how to apply and required documents please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Accompanying-family-members (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Accompanying-family-members)
Work
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work)
Researchers
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Researcher (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Researcher)
Trainee
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Traineeer (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Trainee)
Study
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Study (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Study)
Internship
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Interns (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Interns)
Au Pair
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Au-pair (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Au-pair)
Religious Worker
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Religous-worker (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Religous-worker)
Faroe Islands and Greenland
For details on how to apply and required documents, please check following link: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/The-Faroe-Islands-and-Greenland (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/The-Faroe-Islands-and-Greenland)
Note that this type of application is not available online. It must be handed in at VFS and the hand-in application checklist above is applicable.
APPLICATIONS MADE IN DENMARK
For applications made in Denmark, applicants receive letters requesting biometrics (not to be confused with online application receipts) or decisions. In such situations, the following checklist is applicable when going to VFS: Biometrics Checklist and Decisions Checklist

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
DIS and SIRI have set maximum expected processing times for different application types. This period is specified under each respective application type and you can check it from the links given under ‘documents required’.
